docs.title: Channel Rules
docs.text:
• Post only announcements in the announcements channels.
  • Do not delete channels.
  • If a channel needs removal/replacement:
    1. Move it to the Archived category.
    2. Sync permissions with the category.
    3. Create a new replacement channel. This also includes channels with no messages in them, they might be used by a bot.

docs.title: Staff Expectations
docs.text:
• You are not required 24/7.
  • If you’ll be inactive for a while, let us know.
  • You are trusted with correct permissions - you don’t need approval for actions. If you are unsure, feel free to wait for a higher-up.
  • If you think there might be an error in the permissions, please ask about it in a Staff Ticket.

docs.title: Discord Commands
docs.text:
Written By Didi. All commands here are run from inside Discrd.

Keep in mind of the following:

  • You can ping a user instead of using (username) in most cases, if it doesn't work. Try with their
  • Omit the brackets when running commands
  • If a command starts with - then it is running on the Minecraft server, through @SpaceBot#6456
  • If a command starts with . it is running through @AstroBot#0625, the servers utility and moderation bot Some commands require a Message ID. You have to enable developer mode to get the option to show the "Copy Message ID" button, which will show in the right click menu of a message.

docs.title: Restricting a user
docs.text: Use Case: To prevent a user from logging into the server and talking/viewing any channel except for tickets. .restrict (username)

docs.title: Warning a user
docs.text: Use Case: To give a strike to a user, 3 strikes will ban a user, with the option to appeal. Another strike will permanently ban with no option to appeal. .warn (username) [number of strikes to give, defaults to 1 if nothing is added in this field] (reason)

docs.title: Unwarning a user
docs.text: Use Case: Well what do you think .warnings (username) To see the warnings that a user has. Copy the string of numbers corresponding to the warning you want to remove, and then run .unwarn (username) (string of numbers from above)

docs.title: View warnings
docs.text: Use Case: View how many warnings a user has .warnings (username)

docs.title: Purging messages
docs.text: Use Case: Deleting lots of messages in bulk .cpurge (amount of messages to delete) OR .purge (message id of the last message to keep)

docs.title: Sending messages as AstroBot
docs.text:
Use Case: Staying anonymous in a ticket .say (channel) (message) Please Note: The above command doesnt work with threads. You can use the following, inside the ticket channel as an alternative. .say (message).

If you plan on sending lots of messages, run the following command, inside the channel you would like to send messages in. .interact A session will start inside the Bot's DM's where you can send messages to be sent inside the channel Please note that you should run the command before adding the user to the ticket as they might have a message logger

docs.title: Minecraft Commands
docs.text:
Written By Glory, edits by Didi! All commands here are run from inside Minecraft.

Key:

  • []: Choose from the options, seperated by a comma.
  • (): Required
  • <>: Choose from the provided list

Omit the brackets when running commands

docs.title: Kicking a user
docs.text: Use Case: To temporarily remove a player from the server (until they rejoin) /kick (username)

docs.title: Vanishing
docs.text:
Use Case: To appear offline and invisible so no one knows you're online (you can spy on potential cheaters this way!)

/vanish

Works well with Changing Game Mode

docs.title: Ledger Inspect
docs.text:
/ledger inspect [on/off]

Example use:

  1. Do /ledger inspect on to turn on the inspect feature.
  2. Punch a block
  3. View in chat who broke a block in that spot, who placed one, what block was placed/broken (if you punch a chest it will tell you who added items from the chest and who removed items from it)
  4. Do /ledger inspect off to turn off the inspect feature.

docs.title: Ledger Rollback & Restore
docs.text:
/ledger preview [rollback, restore] <parameters> Depending on if you are happy with what the preview shows, run this command /ledger preview [cancel, apply] Parameters available here

The ledger preview command only supports blocks. For mobs and other actions, drop the preview parts of the commands. Rollback: Restores the world as defined by parameters Restore: Undoes Rollback

Example use

  1. /ledger preview rollback source:eatyourglory range:10 (This command will delete ALL interactions the user eatyourglory had within 10 blocks)
